MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-556980, CGC-18-571649 WITH CASE CGC-16-551921
Off Calendar Jun-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Samuel Cheadle, Esq. 415-381-5599 of Chavez & Gertler LLP for Plaintiff.
David Kleczek, Esq. 510-663-7100 of Kleczek Law Office for Plaintiff through Court Call.
William Bogden, Esq. 415-913-4941 of LeClair Ryan for Defendant Hines G5.
Noah Woo, Esq. 415-781-7072 of Walsworth for Defendant H.V. Welker.
Terri Crawford, Esq. 650-989-5400 of Clapp Moroney Vucinich Beeman Scheley for Defendant Turner Construction.
Ulla Pajala, Esq. 510-444-6800 of Burnham Brown for Defendant California Drywall.
Sadaf Nejat, Esq. 714-634-2522 of WFBM LLP for Cross Defendant National Air Balance through Court Call.
Ha Eun Cho, Esq. 949-252-0550 of London Fischer LLP for Cross Defendant Marelich Mechanical through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF PATRICIA HUDNALL MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-556980, CGC-18-571649 WITH CASE CGC-16-551921.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiffs' motion to consolidate Case Nos. CGC 16-551921, 17-556980 and 18-571649 per CCP 1048 is GRANTED. Common questions of fact and law are pending before the court in these cases brought by four Environmental Protection Agency employees. All allege maladies from airborne toxins in their place of work at 75 Hawthorne Plaza related to a large-scale renovation. Plaintiffs might have moved earlier, but judicial economy will be served and inconsistent adjudications avoided, and consolidation is discretionary. (Todd-Stenberg v. Dalkon Shield Claimants Trust (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 976, 978.)

Defendants argue distinct exposures and symptoms, but that goes without saying as plaintiffs are distinct people. Given the high intelligence of San Francisco jurors, they would not be confused by four plaintiffs, were the consolidated cases ever to be tried. Defendants also argue that the status of discovery varies among the three cases, but show no real prejudice.
Consolidation is for all purposes; 16-551921 is the lead case. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
